title: Red Hat Security Advisory: postgresql16 security update
overview:
An update for postgresql16 is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

affected_products: Red Hat Enterprise Linux CodeReady Linux Builder (v. 10)
full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: postgresql16 security update

An update for postgresql16 is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

An update for postgresql16 is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.

CVE-2025-12818: A vulnerability has been identified in PostgreSQL’s libpq client library, where integer wraparound in several allocation-size calculations allows a peer or input provider to cause an undersized buffer and then write out-of-bounds by hundreds of megabytes. This can lead to a client application segmentation fault or crash when using libpq to connect to a PostgreSQL server.
